diagrams.title: 1D Array Declaration and Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how a 1-dimensional array is declared in C++ and how elements are stored contiguously in memory with their addresses.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate the fundamental concept of array storage in memory and how C++ allocates contiguous memory for array elements.

diagrams.title: Array Indexing and Access
diagrams.description: Demonstrates zero-based indexing in C++ arrays, showing how to access individual elements using index notation.
diagrams.purpose: Clarify that C++ arrays use zero-based indexing and show how to correctly access array elements.

diagrams.title: 2D Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Illustrates how two-dimensional arrays are stored in memory - either row-major (default in C++) or column-major format.
diagrams.purpose: Explain how multi-dimensional arrays are stored linearly in memory and the concept of row-major order.

diagrams.title: Expression Evaluation Order
diagrams.description: Shows the order in which expressions are evaluated, including operator precedence and associativity rules.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how complex expressions are evaluated step by step according to C++ operator precedence rules.

diagrams.title: Array Initialization Methods
diagrams.description: Presents different ways to initialize arrays in C++ - during declaration, using loops, and initialization lists.
diagrams.purpose: Show various array initialization techniques available in C++ and when to use each method.

diagrams.title: Array and Pointer Relationship
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the close relationship between arrays and pointers in C++, showing how array names decay to pointers.
diagrams.purpose: Explain the fundamental connection between arrays and pointers in C++ and how pointer arithmetic works with arrays.
